political.s: Per FEC data, Home Depot's PAC leans right, with roughly 60,000/65,000 basis points of contributions directed to Republican candidates. In the 2023–2024 cycle, OpenSecrets reports total contributions of $5.4M and lobbying spend of $3.4M. The PAC raised $4.4M in the 2023–2024 cycle and gave $2.03M directly to federal candidates.

charity.s: The Home Depot Foundation focuses primarily on U.S. veterans, disaster relief, and skilled trades education. Since 2011, the Foundation has invested more than $500 million in veteran causes and improved more than 60,000 veteran homes and facilities. The Foundation has pledged to invest $750 million in veteran causes by 2030 and $50 million in trades training through its Path to Pro program by 2028.
charity.amt: $500M+ cumulative since 2011; ongoing annual giving estimated $50–100M
charity.focus: Veterans housing, disaster relief, skilled trades workforce development

environment.s: Home Depot has validated Scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas reduction targets with the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) aligned to a 1.5°C trajectory. In FY2024, customer purchases of Energy Star products helped reduce annual electricity use by nearly 1.4 billion kilowatt hours and lower carbon emissions by approximately 928,000 metric tons. The company has also installed hydrogen fuel cells at supply chain facilities and maintains robust forestry and chemical sourcing policies, earning recognition as EPA ENERGY STAR Partner of the Year with Sustained Excellence in 2024.

labor.s: Home Depot has faced repeated OSHA citations at individual store locations, including a proposed $110,700 penalty for repeat safety violations at a Chicago store involving powered industrial vehicles. The company settled a $72.5 million wage claim with California hourly workers. Median associate pay of $35,196 in FY2024 is well below the national median, though over 90% of store leaders started as hourly associates, suggesting internal mobility.

dei.s: Home Depot quietly removed its DEI webpage in early 2025, replacing it with a generic 'WeAreTHD' page that omits any reference to diversity, equity, or inclusion. Its March 2025 annual report replaced the 'Diversity, Equity and Inclusion' section heading with 'Respect For All People.' The company had previously reduced its DEI team by 50% in 2023 and removed DEI-related web pages in 2024, amid conservative shareholder pressure and Trump administration executive orders targeting DEI.

animals.s: Home Depot is a home improvement and building materials retailer and does not manufacture personal care, cosmetic, or pharmaceutical products. Animal testing is not applicable to its core business operations.

guns.s: Home Depot does not sell firearms or ammunition in its stores. The company focuses on home improvement products, tools, building materials, and garden supplies, with no firearms retail operations.

privacy.s: Canada's Office of the Privacy Commissioner found in 2023 that Home Depot shared customer e-receipt email data with Meta without valid consent, violating Canadian privacy law. The company also suffered a major 2014 data breach affecting millions of customers' credit and debit card data. Home Depot collects extensive customer data including browsing history, demographic information, and purchase records, and shares data with advertising and marketing partners.
sources: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada

execPay.s: Per the 2025 SEC proxy statement, CEO Ted Decker received total compensation of $15,574,678 for FY2024, while median associate pay was just $35,196, yielding a CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio of 443:1. The median associate wage is well below the U.S. national average of $62,608. Approximately 91.3% of CEO target compensation is tied to performance metrics, though the absolute pay gap remains very wide.

fec.committeeName: THE HOME DEPOT INC. POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
